Additional Note: Includes 6x BO60 60RPM motors, wheels, rocker-bogie frame and assembly manual. SG90 servo and ultrasonic sensor included in kit are not used in this project.
360 degree laser scanner with 8m range. Connects to Raspberry Pi via USB. Must be mounted at highest point of rover with clear 360 degree view.
Main mapping computer running Ubuntu Server 22.04 and ROS2 Humble. Requires 16GB+ microSD card. Apply heatsinks before first boot.
Motor controller. Hosts its own WiFi access point and WebSocket server for browser-based rover control.
Channel A (OUT1+OUT2) drives right 2 motors. Channel B (OUT3+OUT4) drives left 2 motors. Connect ESP32 GND to L298N GND.
6-axis gyroscope and accelerometer. Connects to Raspberry Pi via I2C (SDA pin 3 and SCL pin 5). Must be mounted flat and level.
Powers motors via L298N. Keep completely isolated from Raspberry Pi power circuit. Secure low on chassis for lower center of gravity.
Powers Raspberry Pi via USB. Must output 5V at 2.5A minimum. Using a separate powerbank prevents motor voltage spikes from corrupting the Pi.

Name of the Material/Component: Aluminium Heatsink Set for Raspberry Pi
Additional Note: Apply largest heatsink to main processor chip and medium heatsink to USB/Ethernet controller chip before mounting Pi on chassis.
Use to mount all PCBs above metal chassis. Never place circuit boards directly on metal chassis as it causes short circuits.
Main battery connector. Solder to battery leads and power wires going to L298N.
OS storage for Raspberry Pi. Class 10 or U3 minimum. Faster cards reduce boot time.
One for Powerbank to Raspberry Pi. One for ESP32 programming via Arduino IDE. Must support data transfer not just charging.
Connects YDLidar X2 USB adapter board to Raspberry Pi USB port.
For motor and power connections between battery, L298N and motors.
For signal connections between ESP32 and L298N. Also for MPU-6050 to Raspberry Pi I2C connections.
Custom mounts for YDLidar X2 and component brackets. STL files in the CAD folder of this repo. Print in PLA at 20% infill minimum.

Name of the Material/Component: Zip Ties and Double-Sided Foam Tape
Additional Note: For cable management and securing components to the chassis frame.
